description: Normal-road lead-braking rear-end threat: a lead vehicle in the ego lane hard-brakes onto a collision course while a follower is close behind ego. The right corridor is blocked, leaving only a left evasive corridor, so the sweep tests braking versus left lane-change/T-drift under rear-impact pressure.
hypothesis: Full braking should dominate at higher TTC or larger follower gaps, while left evasive maneuvering should dominate at lower TTC, higher ego speed, and tight follower gaps where rear-end risk makes braking costly.
